mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the effective federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value equity prices and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The reverse holds for a sustained rise.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.42 in Feb-2018 vintage, near post-crisis lows entering hiking cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign of correlation flips for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals an inflection in labor market deterioration that investors have not yet priced, triggering a short-horizon risk-off repricing of equities over the following 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE flat at 4.1% as of Feb-2018 vintage, low and stable
failure_condition: SPX produces non-negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or effect is not distinguishable from baseline unconditional distribution at p>0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) year-over-year growth exceeds 4% simultaneously with housing starts (HOUST) year-over-year growth exceeding 5%, the joint strength in consumer and residential investment demand signals broad-based economic momentum that supports equity risk appetite over the next 20 trading days. Neither series alone is sufficient; only the joint condition reliably reflects synchronized demand-side strength.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage 492008 vs revised 484031 Feb-2018, HOUST vintage 1326 vs revised 1299 Jan-2018
failure_condition: Joint-trigger sample shows forward 20d return mean not statistically greater than unconditional sample mean (t-test p>0.05) over any 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of negative month-over-month INDPRO growth signal an emerging industrial-sector contraction that historically precedes broader earnings deceleration, leading to below-average S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter (60 trading days).
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth negative for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value 107.2433 vs revised_value 101.4625 Jan-2018 showing large revision risk
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return conditional on the two-month contraction trigger is not significantly below the unconditional 60-day mean (p>0.05) across at least two independent decades in the sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the nominal federal funds rate rises faster than CPI inflation such that the implied real policy rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL year-over-year change) exceeds 1.5 percentage points, monetary policy is restrictively tight relative to inflation, compressing valuation multiples and depressing S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. This effect requires both series moving jointly; FEDFUNDS alone does not capture real tightness.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL 249.619 vintage vs 249.529 revised Feb-2018, FEDFUNDS 1.42 stable
failure_condition: Rank IC between the real-rate proxy and forward 60d returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window during periods when the trigger is active, or direction flips sign in more than one distinct tightening episode.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) over the trailing 3 months reflects continued labor demand expansion and corporate confidence, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days via sustained income and consumption channels.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage 148177 vs revised 148054 Feb-2018 showing modest revision
failure_condition: Correlation between trailing 3-month PAYEMS growth and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 rank IC over any rolling 2-year window, or sign inverts across two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) quarter-over-quarter annualized growth decelerates below 1.5%, aggregate demand momentum has weakened enough to foreshadow slower corporate earnings growth, producing below-average S&P 500 returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage 17271.702 vs revised 19882.352 Oct-2017 showing extreme revision risk in this series
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return conditional on sub-1.5% GDP growth trigger is not significantly below unconditional mean (p>0.05), or high revision risk causes trigger classification to reverse in more than 30% of instances upon later vintage comparison.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle regime where UNRATE is below 4.5% (indicating a tight labor market) while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously rising, the market interprets continued tightening against a low-slack backdrop as a signal of increased recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days around policy-sensitive data releases. Neither low unemployment nor rate hikes alone reliably triggers this effect.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% and FEDFUNDS at 1.42% rising through Feb-2018 vintage, consistent with tightening cycle amid tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces non-negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of joint-trigger instances, or the joint condition shows no significant difference from single-factor UNRATE or FEDFUNDS triggers alone (p>0.10).
